title: Akagi ladies thought Lilith was Adam Eva 00 from Lilith
display_title: Akagi ladies thought Lilith was Adam Eva 00 from Lilith
wikitext:
[]: Akagi ladies thought Lilith was Adam? / Eva-00 from Lilith?             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 10:39 am As per the title. Here is the reasoning I am proposing , evidence in italic or in spoilers. a) Ritsuko says that man resurrected the God they had found in Antarctica and then lost during 2I. The result was something she calls Adam. Ep 23 - RITSUKO: [?] That was fifteen years ago. The God they found had also disappeared by then. But then, they tried to resurrect God on their own. The result was Adam. b) But we know that if there the first thing to be part of the game and to look like a resurrection of the God of the White Moon, that's for sure Lilith Ep 16 - Kaji: Kaji: Yes, this is the origin and linchpin of everything since the Second Impact. This is Adam. Ep 16 - Misato: Adam? The First Angel is here? You're right. Nerv is deeper in this than I thought. c) Ritsuko says that man created Evas from the-so-called-Adam. Ep 23 - RITSUKO: And imitating God, they created people from Adam. The result was the Evas. d) Also, Ritsuko must have known that Eva-01 came from a pasty giant, most likely Naoko told her since she was present when Eva-01 and the pasty giant were still connected. [no evidence here, but it's very likely, I guess?] e) But we know that the pasty giant that Eva-01 came from is Lilith SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Oct 09, 2013 10:45 am IRCC she was referring to Adam as "the God that was found", not as any sort of result. (other than Second Impact) The "ressurection" of Adam are the Adam-based EVAs themselves. Unit one is special ans referenced as such in a lot of Dialogue, including the one where SEELE calls it Lillith's offspring, none of which includes EVA 00.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 09, 2013 10:51 am Interesting idea, but remember that Eva-01 wasn't created from Lilith alone. It's identical to all the other Evas apart from being made from Lilith's flesh, and aside from being grown from Lilith, it's an Adam-Derived Organism. It's been all but confirmed that the reason for Eva-01 being a "test type" is that instead of being grown from Adam's DNA artificially, Eva-01 was created out of Lilith's flesh using Adam's DNA. Ritsuko probably knows the difference between Adam and Lilith - in fact, a lot of people probably do, given that one of the L's in L.C.L. stands for Lilith. Misato is in the dark, but she has data in EoE that tells her about the existence of Adam and Lilith. Whether or not Kaji was wilfully lying about Lilith being Adam is an open question - but it is his research that helps her establish that Lilith, not Adam, was on the cross. You have Keel's statement in EoE - that Eva-01 is Lilith no bunshin and that Eva alone is considered as a Lilith substitute. Eva-00 had been blown up by that point but nowhere does Seele treat Eva-00 as special in the same way as they treat Eva-01. Finally, Rei is Lilith. Put a piece of Lilith back inside a body created by Lilith - and that's asking for some serious trouble. If Eva-00 had been created out of Lilith, the entire Rei situation would have played out different. Kaworu was able to use Eva-02 like a puppet because it was created from his old body. Had Eva-00 been created out of Lilith, Rei could have turned it into a substitute body and used it to shoot Gendo's plans to hell.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:14 am I have re-read Reichu's commentary on the matter and yes, her translation is different, she doesn't use "the result was Adam" but rather says "that was Adam" in a "and by the way, the South Pole giant was Adam". Ursus, thanks for pointing out a number of things, most of them new to me although I have recently gone through all the platinum transcripts with great care... UrsusArctos wrote: It's been all but confirmed that the reason for Eva-01 being a "test type" is that instead of being grown from Adam's DNA artificially, Eva-01 was created out of Lilith's flesh using Adam's DNA.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:23 am UrsusArctos wrote: Ritsuko probably knows the difference between Adam and Lilith - in fact, a lot of people probably do, given that one of the L's in L.C.L. stands for Lilith .

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:27 am NemZ wrote: Is that actually stated anywhere at all within the series? Because I sure don't recall that as anything but speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:31 am Regarding LCL, Ursus wasn't too far off From the LCL page of the Wiki It is unknown what the initials "LCL" stand for, but we do know what they don't mean. According to the Evangelion: Death and Rebirth theatrical program (special edition): "Incidentally, the widely circulated idea that L.C.L is the abbreviation of "Link Connected Liquid" is incorrect." However, nowhere does it say what it does mean. Why they would go out of their way to say what it doesn't stand for, but not go on to explain what it does stand for, is unknown, and a cause of much fan frustration. Evangelion Chronicle suggests that one of the Ls stands for "Lilith". In the Manga the Dossier at the beginning of a chapter states that LCL does mean "Link Connected Liquid".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:34 am UrsusArctos wrote: You're right, sorry...it was Newtype's speculation that one of the L's in L.C.L. stood for Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:41 am Reichu wrote: It's an old fanwank of mine to explain the fact that Eva-01 looks like Adam and the other Evas, and absolutely nothing like Lilith. Of course, if Lilith's blubbery white form is not her original one, i.e., she originally looked the same as Adam, then Eva-01 being Lilith's clone can make sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:51 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: But Adam au naturel doesn't look like Eva-01 either.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 09, 2013 11:52 am Since Adam looks so much like Lilith in that shot, I have the impression that it was Adam , not Lilith, whose form changed. (Yes, I must've seen that shot a gazillion times but the idea never occured to me)

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 12:03 pm I don't think there is any need to explain the fact that Eva-01 doesn't look much like Lilith. It did when they were attached, Nerv clearly tampered with it later on. If Eva-00 and Eva-02 got modified to have respectively no eyes and 4 eyes when Adam looked like it had two, I take it Nerv can change skin and blood colour as they please or that those changes might be unwanted and unalterable consequences of other changes they had to do for an Eva to be an Eva?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Oct 09, 2013 12:10 pm Why Evas look the way they are is never, ever explained. Eva-02 with its four eyes and Sachiel-style purplish blood is weird as hell, the harpies with their whale heads are weirder (and the weirdness progression goes on in other continuities like Rebuild). Eva-00 has two eyes under that helmet, by the way. Check the Evangelion Biological Listing on the wiki. The other changes make very little sense, and its hard to say how they came about. From all the corpses in the Eva graveyard and skeletons lying about, and artificial spine sections, it looks like they were trying to grow the Evas in segments and assemble them together. After all, they even talk about regrowing parts and cannibalizing parts from the unbuilt Eva-05 and Eva-06 to rebuild Eva-02 and Eva-00 after the entire Zeruel dustup. The only Eva that is confirmed to have been created directly out of a Seed of Life is Eva-01 out of Lilith, and that happens to be the only Eva whose creation method is seen.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 12:13 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Why Evas look the way they are is never, ever explained.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 12:19 pm Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: I don't think there is any need to explain the fact that Eva-01 doesn't look much like Lilith. It did when they were attached, Nerv clearly tampered with it later on.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 1:04 pm Reichu wrote: In other words, an Eva (or one of the pieces used to make it, anyway...) comes out of the vat in its biologically finished form. If you wanted to make changes, you should have induced them while the relevant body parts were growing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 1:31 pm Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: According to what approved textbook of the laws of biology applying to made up humanoids that are derived from godlike life sources that were sent across the universe from a primordial race/civilisation billions of years ago?

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Oct 09, 2013 2:32 pm Well, for sure you have provided me with a great free tour in the fantasyland of "there's a bit of Adam in Shogoki", so thank you for the explanations, I mean it. From your last post, I think I realise I'm not cut for that level of speculation/toying around/fanwank although a lot of the forum is about it. My bad. It might change as the post count goes up.

[]:             Quote Post by Rpahut » Thu Oct 31, 2013 3:58 pm Basically, because Ritsuko doesn't say that Evas came from both Adam and Lilith, but she must have known that Eva-01 came from the pasty giant, I think we can conclude that she believes Lilith is Adam. If that is her knowledge, it is likely that this knowledge doesn't go against anything Naoko must have told her. Therefore, Naoko thought that the giant in the Nerv HQ was a resurrected Adam. Therefore, when Naoko said to Fuyu that Eva-00 was made from Adam, that Adam she means was in fact Lilith. Therefore, Eva-00 is made from Lilith, not Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Thu Oct 31, 2013 4:38 pm Yes but one of my hypothesis relied on this critical Ritsuko line: "But then, they tried to resurrect God on their own. The result was Adam." The last bit, Reichu translated differently in the commentary and in her case it's something like "But then, they tried to resurrect God on their own. And by the way, the God they had found and then lost is what we call Adam." I think it's valuable to add that other translations I know off (the Italian one) seem to be closer to "The result was Adam" but it's really a matter of interpretation, so I find it inconclusive. Finally, Eva-00 being made from Lilith rather than Adam is kind of a dead end anyway, determining its origins doesn't shed light on anything else, it doesn't change anything. But I didn't realise until the topic developed.
